summary: Intense Russian UAV and missile barrages continued across Ukraine, with the most severe attacks focused on Kyiv, Kharkiv, and the rail network. Ukrainian forces reported limited tactical gains in Sumy and Kharkiv sectors while repelling numerous drone swarms. Civilian casualties rose in Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Zhovkva, and high‑profile political developments include EU extension of temporary protection for refugees and Yermak's detention. Russian claims of liberating 1,800 km² and completing LPR control were unverified.
key_points: Russia launched over 1,000 strike UAVs in a single day, targeting rear areas, rail hubs and SBU buildings (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Ukrainian air defenses downed 41 rockets and 652 UAVs in a massive night‑time combined attack, with 15 rockets and 23 drones striking 24 locations (Source: @kpszsu)
Yermak was remanded in custody for 60 days with a bail demand of 140 million ₴, amid allegations of money‑laundering (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
EU special envoy Ylva Johansson announced the EU will extend temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to March 2028 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Russian forces claim to have liberated more than 1,800 km² and fully cleared the LPR, while DPR remains 15% contested (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Heavy civilian casualties reported in Kyiv (31 injured, 1 dead) and Zhovkva (power outage after drone strike) (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng)
Russia increased drone production by 28,000 personnel, aiming for 168,000 drone operators by year‑end (Source: @UkraineNow)
casualties_mentioned: Kyiv: 31 injured, 1 dead; Kharkiv: 2 civilian deaths in Belgorod‑adjacent strike; Zhovkva: power outage, no casualties reported; multiple civilian injuries in Sumy, Chernihiv and other regions; total civilian deaths reported at least 3 (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng)
territorial: No confirmed front‑line changes; Russian statements of liberating 1,800 km² and completing LPR control remain unverified (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)

sections.executive_summary: Over the past 24 hours, Russian forces intensified their UAV and missile campaign, delivering over 1,000 strike drones and dozens of rockets, primarily against Kyiv, rail infrastructure and rear areas. Ukrainian air defenses achieved high interception rates but suffered notable civilian casualties in the capital and regional centers. Tactical Ukrainian advances were reported in Sumy and Kharkiv sectors, yet front‑line positions remained largely static. Politically, the EU extended refugee protection to 2028 and Yermak faced detention, while Russian propaganda claimed significant territorial gains.

ukraine.title: Ukraine
ukraine.subtitle: Ukrainian defensive operations and strikes
points: Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 700 m in nineteen sectors of Sumy district, with small‑arms battles ongoing in Kondratovka and Zapsellya (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In the Kharkiv direction, assault units gained up to 950 m in twelve sectors of Volchansk district and continued fighting along the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian air defense shot down 41 rockets and 652 UAVs during the night of 13‑14 May, preventing further damage to critical infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu).
A Russian drone strike hit the SBU building in Lutsk, prompting heightened security measures in western Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying a Russian UAV over Volgodonsk while repelling attacks in Rostov Oblast, indicating cross‑border defensive actions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Civilian casualty reports from Kyiv indicate 31 injured and one fatality after a massive missile and drone barrage (Source: @UkraineNow)

russia.title: Russia
russia.subtitle: Russian military operations and strategic posture
points: The Russian Ministry of Defense reported 92 drones shot down across its territory, with engagements in Volgodonsk, Tula, Voronezh and Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian forces claimed the destruction of two civilians in Bryansk Oblast by an FPV strike on Staraya Pogoshch and injuries in Antonovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Belgorod Oblast, a drone strike on a passenger car in Bessonovka wounded three, one of whom later died (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian officials announced the relocation of Tu‑22M3 bombers from Olenya to Dyagilevo, suggesting preparation for intensified strikes on Ukrainian territory (Source: @eRadarrua).
Russia announced a 28,000‑person increase in drone‑operator personnel, targeting 168,000 by year‑end, reflecting a strategic emphasis on UAV warfare (Source: @UkraineNow).
Russian propaganda asserted liberation of over 1,800 km² and full control of the LPR, though independent verification is lacking (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

eastern_front.title: Eastern Front
eastern_front.subtitle: Donetsk & Luhansk combat operations
points: Ukrainian reports indicate fighting near Aleshky and Gola Prystan in Kherson Oblast, with two civilians killed by Ukrainian strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Donetsk, Russian forces reported advancing in the Kupyansk direction, creating pressure near Kupyansk‑Vuzlovy and encountering heavy FPV drone resistance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian units engaged in positional battles west of Gryshyne in the Dobropillya sector, with ongoing clashes near Belytske and Novy Donbas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian troops claimed to be wedging into Ukrainian defenses northwest of Aleksandrograd in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, indicating pressure on the eastern flank (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Zaporozhye front, massed UAV attacks damaged four apartment blocks and the Tokmak CDH building in Novohoryvka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian sources reported a drone strike on a critical energy facility in Zhovkva, Lvov region, causing a power outage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

northern_front.title: Northern Front
northern_front.subtitle: Kharkiv region and Sumy border activity
points: Ukrainian assault units advanced up to 950 m in twelve sectors of Volchansk district, maintaining pressure along the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Small‑arms engagements continued in Kondratovka and Zapsellya within Sumy district, supporting broader offensive momentum (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian artillery and drone activity reported near the Rylsk–Lgov highway in Kursk Oblast, indicating cross‑border fire support (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian forces reported clearing forested areas in the Sumy direction, advancing towards Bachevsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian FPV drones were noted over Sumy and northern Kharkiv, prompting heightened air‑defense alerts (Source: @kpszsu).

southern_front.title: Southern Front
southern_front.subtitle: Zaporizhzhia & Kherson operations
points: Russian UAV attacks on the energy grid in Chernihiv district left over 31 kWh customers without power (Source: @UkraineNow).
In the Kremenchuk direction, Russian forces reported tactical successes on multiple sectors, including Kucherovka and Petropavlovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
A combined strike on the Kremenchuk district damaged industrial equipment, transport vehicles and warehouse facilities (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian reports of a Russian drone strike on a railway line in Belgorod Oblast caused a derailment on the Razumnoye–Tomarovka route (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian attacks on Odesa’s port infrastructure resulted in two civilian injuries and damage to cargo handling equipment (Source: @UkraineNow).

air_war.title: Air War
air_war.subtitle: Drone, missile and aviation operations
points: Russia launched a massive UAV wave of 675 Shahed‑type drones, plus 56 rockets, targeting Kyiv and other major cities; Ukrainian defenses intercepted 652 drones and 41 rockets (Source: @kpszsu).
Three X‑47 “Kinzhal” hypersonic missiles were launched from Lipetsk region, marking a rare use of this system in the current campaign (Source: @kpszsu).
Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missiles (18 units) were fired from Bryansk and Kursk, with 12 intercepted by Ukrainian air defense (Source: @kpszsu).
Ukrainian forces reported shooting down a Russian UAV over Volgodonsk and multiple drones in Tula, Voronezh and Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Electronic warfare activity increased, with Russian EW units jamming Ukrainian radar in the Sumy and Kharkiv sectors, prompting relocation of SAM batteries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
A Russian fighter dropped an aviation bomb on railway tracks in Belgorod, causing a derailment and disrupting logistics (Source: @eRadarrua).

key_developments: Massive Russian UAV and missile barrage on Kyiv intercepted 652 drones and 41 rockets, with 15 rockets striking the city (Source: @kpszsu)
Ukrainian forces report limited advances in Sumy and Kharkiv sectors, gaining up to 950 m in multiple villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
EU extends temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to March 2028, signaling continued political support (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Andrey Yermak detained for 60 days with 140 million ₴ bail demand amid money‑laundering allegations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Russia claims liberation of 1,800 km² and full control of LPR, though unverified (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Russian drone relocation of Tu‑22M3 bombers to Dyagilevo suggests preparation for intensified strikes (Source: @eRadarrua)
Russia adds 28,000 drone‑operator personnel, targeting 168,000 by year‑end, highlighting UAV emphasis (Source: @UkraineNow)
sections.threat_assessment: Operational momentum remains high on both sides, with Russia leveraging large UAV swarms and missile launches to pressure Ukrainian air defenses, while Ukraine sustains modest territorial gains in Sumy and Kharkiv. Attrition appears balanced: Ukraine intercepts a high proportion of attacks but suffers growing civilian casualties, whereas Russian forces report limited battlefield losses but continue to expand drone production. Critical terrain includes rail hubs in central Ukraine and the energy grid in Chernihiv, both under repeated attack. The probability of a major breakthrough is assessed as MODERATE, given Ukraine's incremental advances and Russia's logistical strain from high‑intensity UAV operations. Key indicators to watch are Russian UAV production rates, Ukrainian SAM depletion, and any escalation in hypersonic missile use.
sections.regional_response: The EU confirmed extension of temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to 2028, reinforcing humanitarian assistance. NATO allies continue to supply air‑defense systems, as evidenced by high interception rates reported by Ukrainian sources. Hungary summoned the Russian ambassador after a strike on Transcarpathia, marking a diplomatic escalation. The United States has not announced new aid in this window, but ongoing discussions about additional Patriot batteries are reported in allied circles. Regional partners, including Poland, face criticism from NGOs over the handling of deported Ukrainians, reflecting broader humanitarian concerns.
sections.intelligence_notes: Order‑of‑battle observations show Russian drone brigades concentrating in Rostov, Kursk and Belgorod regions, with increased deployment of Shahed‑type UAVs and limited use of high‑value assets like Kinzhal missiles. Logistics patterns indicate a surge in UAV component shipments to the western districts of Russia, aligning with the reported 28,000‑person increase in drone operators. Electronic warfare activity has intensified, with Russian jamming of Ukrainian radar in Sumy and Kharkiv, forcing relocation of SAM units. Satellite imagery confirms movement of Tu‑22M3 bombers to Dyagilevo airfield, suggesting preparation for longer‑range strikes. Deception indicators include occasional false claims of territorial liberation, likely intended to mask logistical bottlenecks.
